Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 085. A Messenger Returns from Birat (ABL 0396)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ia

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭab-ṣill-Ešarra. Good health to the king, my lord! May Aššur and Mullissu bless the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mDÙG.GA—ṣil—É.ŠÁR.RA

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ía*

o 44

aš-šur dNIN.LÍL a-na LUGAL

o 55

EN-ia lik-ru-bu

o 66

mḫal-di—PAB LÚv.rak-su

(6) Haldi-naṣir, the mercenary who took the letter to the Biratean, has come back carrying a letter; [I am sending him] right now [to] the king, my lord.

o 77

ša e-gír-tú

o 88

ina UGU KUR.bi-ra-ta-a.a

o 99

ú-bi-lu-ni

o 1010

i-tal-ka e-gír-tú na-ṣa

o 1111

an-⸢nu⸣-rig

o 1212

[ina pa-an] LUGAL EN-ia

o 1313

[a-sap-ra-áš-šú]

Bottom
b.e. 14b.e. 14

[a-sa-ʾa-al-šú]

Reverse
r 1r 1

[mu-uk] a-ta-a

(r 1) [I asked him]: "Why were you delayed?" He said: "He detained me for 12 days; Marduk-šumu-iddina will be coming right after me."

r 22

tú-ki-iš

r 33

ma-a 12 UD-MEŠ

r 44

ik-ta-al-a-ni

r 55

ma-a mdMES—MU—SUM-na

r 66

an-nu-rig

r 77

i—da-a-tú-u-a

r 88

il-la-ka

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334271/.
